title: Add pagination to user list endpoint
body:
The `/api/users` endpoint currently returns all users in a single response. This causes performance issues when there are more than 1000 users.

## Expected Behavior
The endpoint should support cursor-based pagination with a configurable page size (default 50, max 200).

## Additional Context
This is related to #42 which tracks the overall API performance improvements.
Also see #10 for the database indexing work that needs to happen first.

comments_data.body: We should use cursor-based pagination instead of offset-based to avoid the N+1 problem with large datasets.
Agreed. I'll implement cursor-based pagination using the `id` column as the cursor. Will also add `Link` headers for discoverability.

title: Fix race condition in payment processing
body:
When two payment requests arrive simultaneously for the same order, both can be processed, leading to double charges.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Create an order
2. Send two POST /payments requests at the same time
3. Both succeed instead of one failing

## Root Cause
The payment handler does not use optimistic locking on the order record.

comments_data.body: We need to add a unique constraint on (order_id, status) and use SELECT FOR UPDATE in the transaction. This is a critical fix for production.

title: Add OpenAPI spec generation
body:
We should auto-generate OpenAPI 3.0 specs from our route definitions to keep documentation in sync with the code.

This depends on #15 being completed first since the pagination parameters need to be documented.
